perturbation.original_text: Consultant hereby irrevocably assigns to Company all right, title and interest in and to any information (including, without limitation, business plans and/or business information), technology, know-how, materials, notes, records, designs, ideas, inventions, improvements, devices, developments, discoveries, compositions, trade secrets, processes, methods and/or techniques, whether or not patentable or copyrightable, that are conceived, reduced to practice or made by Consultant alone or jointly with others in the course of performing the Services or through the use of Confidential Information (collectively, 111nventions").
perturbation.changed_text: Consultant hereby assigns to Company all right, title and interest in and to any information (including, without limitation, business plans and/or business information), technology, know-how, materials, notes, records, designs, ideas, inventions, improvements, devices, developments, discoveries, compositions, trade secrets, processes, methods and/or techniques, whether or not patentable or copyrightable, that are conceived, reduced to practice or made by Consultant alone or jointly with others in the course of performing the Services or through the use of Confidential Information (collectively, 111nventions"). However, consultant has the right to revoke the assignments with a 30 days prior written notice.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'irrevocably' and adding a clause allowing revocation with 30 days' notice, the nature of the assignment is fundamentally altered. The original clause indicated a permanent transfer of rights, whereas the modification introduces a contingency that allows the Consultant to reclaim those rights. This change contradicts the intent of Section 3, leading to ambiguity about the ownership of 'Inventions'. It introduces uncertainty about whether Company truly owns the Inventions.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Consultant hereby assigns to Company all right, title and interest in and to any information (including, without limitation, business plans and/or business information), technology, know-how, materials, notes, records, designs, ideas, inventions, improvements, devices, developments, discoveries, compositions, trade secrets, processes, methods and/or techniques, whether or not patentable or copyrightable, that are conceived, reduced to practice or made by Consultant alone or jointly with others in the course of performing the Services or through the use of Confidential Information (collectively, 111nventions"). However, consultant has the right to revoke the assignments with a 30 days prior written notice. Consultant agrees to sign, execute and acknowledge or cause to be signed, executed and acknowledged without cost, but at the expense of Company, any and all documents and to perform such acts as my be necessary, useful or convenient for the purposes of perfecting the foregoing assignments and obtaining, enforcing and defending intellectual property rights in any and all countries with respect to Inventions.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a revocation clause, directly contradicting the original intention of an irrevocable assignment as implied in the unchanged portion of the contradicted text which states: 'Consultant agrees to sign, execute and acknowledge or cause to be signed, executed and acknowledged without cost, but at the expense of Company, any and all documents and to perform such acts as my be necessary, useful or convenient for the purposes of perfecting the foregoing assignments and obtaining, enforcing and defending intellectual property rights in any and all countries with respect to Inventions.' The revocation clause creates uncertainty and undermines the company's ability to 'perfect' and 'enforce' intellectual property rights, thus creating an in-text contradiction as per the definition of Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Services performed hereunder will not be conducted on time that is required to be devoted to any other third party.
perturbation.changed_text: The Services performed hereunder will primarily be conducted on time that is required to be devoted to any other third party.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'will not' to 'will primarily', there is now a contradiction where consultant is using resources that is primary for third party. The original clause aimed to prevent conflicts of interest by ensuring the Consultant's services to the Company were distinct from obligations to other parties. This new clause contradicts with the previous sentence in Section 8, where Consultant has to segregate services and minimize risk.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Subject to written waivers that may be provided by the Company upon request, which shall not be unreasonably withheld, Consultant agrees that, during the term of this Agreement, Consultant will not directly or indirectly (i) participate in the formation of any business or commercial entity in the Field of Interest or otherwise competitive with the Company. Without limiting the foregoing, Consultant agrees to use his or her best efforts (A) to segregate Consultant's 844.KEY.CURE | www.kiromic.com PAGE 3 Services performed under this Agreement from Consultant's work done for any other companies for whom Consultant is providing services so as to minimize any questions of disclosure of, or rights under, any inventions, (B) to notify the Company if at any time the Consultant believes that such questions may result from his or her performance under this Agreement and (C) to assist the Company in fairly resolving any questions in this regard which may arise. The Services performed hereunder will primarily be conducted on time that is required to be devoted to any other third party. The Consultant shall not use the funding, resources and facilities of any other third party, without the prior written consent of the Company, to perform Services hereunder and shall not perform the Services hereunder in any manner that would give any third-party rights or access to the product of such Services.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly stated the consultant's services would *not* be conducted on time required for a third party, ensuring segregation and minimizing conflicts of interest. The changed text now says the services will *primarily* be conducted on that time, directly contradicting the instruction in the contradicted section, which requires the Consultant to 'segregate Consultant's Services performed under this Agreement from Consultant's work done for any other companies for whom Consultant is providing services so as to minimize any questions of disclosure of, or rights under, any inventions'. The change creates a strong contradiction because it now implies the consultant can use time and potentially resources primarily intended for another party, which conflicts with the consultant's responsibility to segregate services and minimize risk of disclosing IP.
